inputs.label: Total Milk Produced (L)
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of milk produced over the measurement period. Essential for yield calculation. Source: Dairy industry standards (IDF).

inputs.label: Total Feed Consumed (kg DM)
inputs.businessContext: Total dry matter feed consumed by the herd. Key input for feed conversion efficiency. Source: NRC dairy nutrition guidelines.

inputs.label: Number of Lactating Cows
inputs.businessContext: Count of cows in lactation during the period. Used to normalize yield per cow. Standard dairy metric.

inputs.label: Measurement Period (days)
inputs.businessContext: Duration over which milk and feed data are collected. Allows daily yield calculation. Common periods: 30 days (monthly), 365 days (annual).

inputs.label: Milk Fat (%)
inputs.businessContext: Average milk fat percentage. Used to calculate energy-corrected milk (ECM) for standardized yield. Reference: IDF standard.

inputs.label: Milk Protein (%)
inputs.businessContext: Average milk protein percentage. Used in ECM calculation. Standard dairy metric.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective confidence in data accuracy. Affects adjusted yield. Low: estimated data; High: measured with calibrated equipment.

hiddenLossDrivers: milkYieldPerCowPerDay < 15 ? 'Low yield per cow: check nutrition, health, or genetics' : ''
feedConversionEfficiency < 0.8 ? 'Poor feed efficiency: evaluate feed quality and ration formulation' : ''
milkYieldPerCowPerDay > 40 ? 'High yield per cow: monitor for metabolic disorders' : ''
suggestedActions: If yield low: review feed ration, check for subclinical mastitis, and assess cow comfort.
If feed efficiency low: analyze feed composition, reduce waste, and consider additives.
If yield high: ensure adequate water intake and monitor body condition score.
